description: Agent Card builder-level wire-shape parity (Algorithm A-CARD). The runner builds an AgentCard via AgentCardBuilder.build(registry, name, description, version, url, capabilities, security_schemes) where `security_schemes` is the FLAT descriptor returned by JWTAuthenticator.security_schemes(), serializes to the A2A 1.0 JSON wire form (camelCase), and partial-matches expected_card. Locks the proto3 securitySchemes oneof shape and supportedInterfaces (no top-level url). NOTE: agent name/version fallback and capabilities.{streaming,pushNotifications} computation live in the serve() layer (not the builder) and are covered by per-SDK TC-AGC unit tests, not by this fixture.

test_cases.description: The flat {type:http,scheme:bearer,bearerFormat:JWT} descriptor is transformed into the A2A 1.0 proto3 oneof shape on the wire (scheme type = the oneof key httpAuthSecurityScheme, NOT a `type` field).

known_divergences: auth.md once wrongly showed securityRequirements as [{"bearerAuth":[]}]; the locked value is always empty. On the JSON wire (proto3) an empty repeated/map field is omitted, so the runner treats an absent securitySchemes/securityRequirements as empty rather than asserting a literal [] / {}.
TS/Rust previously emitted the flat security-scheme descriptor on the wire; fixed via toA2ASecurityScheme / to_a2a_security_schemes so all three now emit the oneof shape asserted here.
provider may be null or an object depending on configuration; not asserted here.
